search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG19 diabetic foot bilateral examination risk assessment follow-up
site:dha.gov.ae type 2 diabetes annual foot review Dubai
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic low, moderate or high foot-risk classification
automatic podiatry, vascular, antibiotic, offloading or urgent referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Risk classification must be explicitly clinician-entered after examination.
No treatment, medicine, procedure or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children, young people and adults with diabetes, with age-specific assessment and active-foot-problem qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Diabetes foot-risk review; active infection, ulcer, ischaemia, gangrene or Charcot concern requires an active-problem pathway.
UAE_applicability: DHA annual-review evidence is UAE-direct for adult Dubai telehealth; NICE foot pathways require UAE podiatry, vascular, infection and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: NICE NG19 and DHA Issue 2 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE bilateral foot-assessment section supports interval change, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, focused foot or skin examination and clinician concern only when assessed; it does not calculate risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact foot-service follow-up section supports previous correspondence, clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only when documented; it does not create a referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA annual-review section supports measured vital signs, weight or BMI, laboratory and home-monitoring review in adult type 2 diabetes without assigning foot risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

unresolved_source_gaps: Ulcer history, skin, callus, deformity, sensation, pulses, infection, Charcot, footwear, clinician risk category, referral, treatment and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
